content:
First write 
 p(x)=A·(x−5)(x−15)(x−25)+q(x), 
where q is a quadratic.  Then p(5)=q(5), p(25)=q(25), and p(15)=q(15).  Write 
 q(x)=u x²+v x+w. 
The condition p(5)+p(25)=1906 becomes  
 q(5)+q(25)=1906  
⇔ (25u+5v+w)+(625u+25v+w)=650u+30v+2w=1906  
⇔ 325u+15v+w=953.  
But  
 p(15)=q(15)=225u+15v+w,  
so  
 p(15)−(325u+15v+w)=−100u  
⇒ p(15)=953−100u.  
To minimize |953−100u| over integers u, we choose u=10, giving p(15)=−47.  Hence the minimum possible value of |p(15)| is 

\boxed{47}.

message.content:
First write 
 p(x)=A·(x−5)(x−15)(x−25)+q(x), 
where q is a quadratic.  Then p(5)=q(5), p(25)=q(25), and p(15)=q(15).  Write 
 q(x)=u x²+v x+w. 
The condition p(5)+p(25)=1906 becomes  
 q(5)+q(25)=1906  
⇔ (25u+5v+w)+(625u+25v+w)=650u+30v+2w=1906  
⇔ 325u+15v+w=953.  
But  
 p(15)=q(15)=225u+15v+w,  
so  
 p(15)−(325u+15v+w)=−100u  
⇒ p(15)=953−100u.  
To minimize |953−100u| over integers u, we choose u=10, giving p(15)=−47.  Hence the minimum possible value of |p(15)| is 

\boxed{47}.
